name: OFFSIDE — The Disagreement Pipeline
description: Docling evidence → per-lens retrieval → four Granite lens readings → Granite Guardian groundedness gate → deterministic SPLIT routing → frozen fixture. The orchestration OFFSIDE runs at bake time, as a Langflow graph.

node.description: IBM Docling extracts the IFAB Laws of the Game into structured, page-cited evidence (page number + bounding box + verbatim text). This is the click-to-source spine.

granite-embedding:30m embeds every evidence atom into a per-lens LanceDB index. Each lens sees only its own evidence via a metadata filter — disagreement emerges because the retrieved evidence differs, not because tone differs.
Granite reads only the IFAB Law evidence. Cite-or-die: every sentence cites a real clause, or the lens returns INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE. No numbers, ever.
Granite reads only the StatsBomb event aggregate. Reports where the data model strains (a goal forced into the catch-all body-part label 'Other'); never a stat value.
Granite reads only the curated historical record. Reconstructs what was knowable AT THE MOMENT of the decision versus now — the decision-time deficit.
Granite reads only named-source quotes. Reports what each NAMED person said; a nationality is never a source. The structural guard against stereotyping.
Granite Guardian — a SECOND IBM model — audits each lens reading for groundedness against its cited page. Any reading it cannot confirm is demoted to INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE. The build-time trust gate.

The gated lens evidence is routed onto the four MECE axes (Rule Ambiguity, Indeterminacy, Decision-Time Deficit, Cultural Prior Bias) by fixed deterministic rules. THE SPLIT carries states, never magnitudes.
The audited bundle is frozen to byte-identical JSON with full provenance (models + corpus git SHA). The web app reads this offline — no model runs at runtime.
